    docs(027/004): restore useful MCP config notes, trim only the trivia
    
    Removing all _NOTE_* keys was too aggressive. Restore the ~17 operational and
    reference notes across the four runtime configs, canonicalised to the correct
    "Node" hf-local wording and aligned 1:1 per server. Trim only the drift-prone
    trivia: the per-server token-budget notes (_NOTE_CONTEXT_BUDGET x4 +
    _NOTE_TOTAL_MCP_BUDGET) and the full tool-list dumps (_NOTE_2_TOOLS x3). The
    _NOTE_DAEMON_REELECTION note is reworded for the code-default-on reality.
    
    Keeps the structural fixes from c67a972b88 (valid JSON, alphabetised, legacy
    env renamed, redundant reelection entry gone, doc-triggers aligned). The
    mk_code_index INDEX_* committed defaults stay "false" per their own
    _NOTE_3_INDEX_DEFAULTS note; the working tree's uncommitted INDEX=true is a
    maintainer-mode override that belongs in the gitignored .env.local.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+        "_NOTE_5_CLOUD_PROVIDERS": "Cloud tiers are explicit opt-in: add VOYAGE_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY only when you want cloud embeddings; otherwise the cascade keeps everything local. Voyage is embeddings-only here; cloud rerankers (voyage/cohere) were removed in 022/013.",

       }

commit c67a972b88265925e07bc2273620fc09c3f9a500
Author: Michel Kerkmeester <82775228+MichelKerkmeester@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Sun Jun 14 09:00:06 2026 +0200

    feat(027): daemon re-election on by default + MCP config 1:1 alignment
    
    Flip the launcher code default for SPECKIT_DAEMON_REELECTION from off to on
    (daemonReelectionEnabled now returns true unless explicitly '0'/'off'), so the
    shared daemon outlives its owning session by default rather than only when a
    runtime config opts in. Invert the unit test's default assertion accordingly
    (5/5 green) and sync the living docs (ENV_REFERENCE, README, feature_catalog,
    playbook) to "on by default in launcher code"; the historical changelog is left
    as the accurate as-of-release record.
    
    Sort + align the four MCP runtime configs 1:1 (.mcp.json -> .claude/mcp.json
    symlink, opencode.json, .codex/config.toml): fix the invalid-JSON missing comma
    in the code-index block, strip all _NOTE_* pseudo-comment env keys, drop the now
    redundant explicit SPECKIT_DAEMON_REELECTION entries (the code default covers
    them), rename legacy SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED to
    MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED, align SPECKIT_ADVISOR_DOC_TRIGGERS across all
    four, and alphabetise each server's env block so every block is byte-identical
    across files.
    
    Embeddings unchanged: Ollama stays preferred via EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER=auto;
    HF_EMBED_SERVER_URL is only the shared hf-local fallback socket, not a provider
    switch.
    
    Spec-folder documentation (027/004-shared-infrastructure) is deferred while a
    concurrent session restructures the 027 spec tree.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-_NOTE_6_GET_VOYAGE_KEY = "Get Voyage key: https://dash.voyageai.com/api-keys"

196: // Daemon re-election (on by default; set SPECKIT_DAEMON_REELECTION=0 or off to restore kill-on-disposal).
197: // When enabled the owner spawns the daemon detached and, on its own shutdown, RELEASES the daemon
198: // (leaves it running for a live secondary to bridge to) instead of killing it, so a session ending does
199: // not tear the shared backend out from under other sessions. A fresh session that finds the released
200: // daemon under a stale lease ADOPTS it through the bridge when the recorded child is alive and bridgeable;
201: // it reaps and respawns only when that daemon is dead or unbridgeable, so the database keeps a single
202: // writer either way. Disabling collapses every path below to the prior behavior (daemon tied to the owner,
203: // killed on shutdown). A released daemon reparents to pid 1, bounded by its idle self-exit, so the orphan
204: // sweeper bounds any leak.
205: function daemonReelectionEnabled(env = process.env) {
206:   return env.SPECKIT_DAEMON_REELECTION !== '0' && env.SPECKIT_DAEMON_REELECTION !== 'off';

214:   return Boolean(enabled) && Boolean(hasLiveDaemon);

Tier 3 LLM save routing is always on (fail-open to Tier 2 on timeout).'), ('_NOTE_DAEMON_REELECTION', 'Daemon re-election is on by default in the launcher code: on disposal the launcher releases the shared daemon for a live secondary to adopt instead of killing it, so concurrent sessions keep MCP transport. Pin to a short /tmp path so the multi-client bridge can bind.')]
.mcp.json mk_skill_advisor [('HF_EMBED_SERVER_URL', 'unix:///tmp/mk-hf-embed/hf-embed.sock'), ('MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR', '.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/database'), ('MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED', '0'), ('MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_TRUST_DEFAULT', 'trusted'), ('SPECKIT_ADVISOR_DOC_TRIGGERS', 'true'), ('SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR', '/tmp/mk-skill-advisor'), ('SPECKIT_MAX_SECONDARY_CLIENTS', '64'), ('_NOTE_1_DB', 'Database lives at .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/database/skill-graph.sqlite by default; MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR overrides, with SYSTEM_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR as a legacy fallback (both still recognized by the launcher + handlers).'), ('_NOTE_3_THRESHOLDS', 'Advisor confidence/uncertainty thresholds are tunable: SPECKIT_ADVISOR_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD (default 0.8), SPECKIT_ADVISOR_UNCERTAINTY_THRESHOLD (default 0.35). Lane weights tunable via SPECKIT_ADVISOR_LANE_WEIGHTS_JSON + SPECKIT_ADVISOR_LANE_SHADOW_WEIGHTS_JSON (partial-merge JSON, post-022/011/4b).'), ('_NOTE_DOC_TRIGGERS', 'Opt-in doc-frontmatter harvest: skill_graph_scan ingests references/assets trigger_phrases into skill_docs and advisor_recommend returns matchedDocs pointers (dampened derived-lane signal). Default-off in code; enabled for this repo. Unset/false to roll back.'), ('_NOTE_HF_EMBED_SOCKET', "Shared hf-local model-server socket — identical to mk-spec-memory's so both services share ONE resident nomic server. (skill-advisor only spawns it when SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_MODEL_SERVER_ENABLED=1; otherwise it just connects to mk-spec-memory's.)"), ('_NOTE_TRUST_DEFAULT', "Daemon-owner trust grant for mutation tools (advisor_rebuild, skill_graph_scan, skill_graph_propagate_enhances). Callers whose _meta omits transport/trust markers default to UNTRUSTED unless this is 'trusted'. Native MCP clients (e.g. /doctor repair flows) send no _meta, so this grant keeps them working; remove it to fail-closed on every transport-absent caller. Explicit _meta trusted:false / callerAuthority:'untrusted' is always rejected.")]
.mcp.json mk_code_index [('SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS', 'false'), ('SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS', 'false'), ('SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS', 'false'), ('SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS', 'false'), ('SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS', 'false'), ('SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR', '/tmp/mk-code-index'), ('SPECKIT_MAX_SECONDARY_CLIENTS', '64'), ('_NOTE_1_DB', 'Database lives SKILL-LOCAL at .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite by default (shared across runtimes via the .opencode/skills symlink); SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR overrides. Former shared location (.opencode/.spec-kit/code-graph/database/) is migrated back on first launcher startup.'), ('_NOTE_3_INDEX_DEFAULTS', 'INDEX_* committed defaults are false (end-user safe). Maintainer mode: set SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true in .env.local (gitignored); the launcher will force all 5 INDEX_* to true at startup. Per-call code_graph_scan args (includeSkills, includeAgents, includeCommands, includeSpecs, includePlugins) still override env for fine-grained control.'), ('_NOTE_4_RESCAN_THRESHOLD', 'SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_SELECTIVE_REINDEX_THRESHOLD (default 50) controls when the launcher switches from selective rescan to full scan. Raise to delay full-scans (faster session-boot, more drift tolerance); lower to keep the DB tighter. Post-commit hook (.opencode/scripts/git-hooks/post-commit) also invalidates the SQLite when commits exceed SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT_REBUILD_THRESHOLD (default 100).')]

  Line 180: | `SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR` | database directory | string | Overrides the daemon IPC socket directory. **Required on macOS** for production runtimes: the default `<service-db>/daemon-ipc.sock` path exceeds the 104-char `sun_path` limit and `listen()` fails with `EINVAL`. Default off — the transport/socket/route subset runs against the real binary either way; the model-path cases auto-skip so default CI is green where no loadable model is available. | `mcp_server/tests/embedders/launcher-model-server-live-two-launcher.vitest.ts` |

  Line 492: ### Local HF model server (single resident model)

  Line 494: When the cascade selects `hf-local`, embeddings are served by a **launcher-supervised local HTTP model server** (`bin/hf-model-server.cjs`) over a Unix socket at `<dbDir>/hf-embed.sock` — no in-process model load, no sidecar. The mk-spec-memory launcher lazily spawns and supervises it on first embed demand; `SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_MODEL_SERVER_ENABLED=1` lets skill-advisor win that spawn when the memory daemon is absent. Both services resolve the **same** socket, so one resident model serves all consumers.

  Line 496: **Single-resident-model contract.** The server loads exactly **one** model (`HF_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL`, default `nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5`). A request for any other model returns **HTTP 404** (`{error, model, loadedModel}`); the `hf-local` provider treats that as "model not loaded" and reports the requested model beside the server's loaded model. To run a different local HF model, change `HF_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL` for **all** consumers — do not expect per-request model switching.

  Line 498: **First-embed download.** On a cold machine, the first hf-local embed downloads the model artifacts to `~/.cache/huggingface/hub`. Expect roughly hundreds of MB of cache growth (about 250-600 MB depending on dtype/artifacts) and a 15-120 s first request on a typical connection. The client keeps retrying while health reports `loading` until `SPECKIT_HF_MODEL_SERVER_LOADING_MAX_MS` (default 150 s).

  Line 500: **Troubleshooting — model-server health states:**

  Line 504: | Not started | `hf-local` health probe connect-refused; no `hf-embed.sock` | Normal before first demand. If it persists under load, check the launcher log for `demand listener` errors; ensure the daemon (or `SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_MODEL_SERVER_ENABLED=1`) is up. |

  Line 506: | Crash-looped | Repeated `hf-model-server child exited … relaunching`; eventually `crash loop detected … daemon remains running` | Inspect the model-server stderr (bad `HF_EMBEDDINGS_DTYPE`/`HF_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL`, OOM). After give-up, the launcher re-arms a demand listener; fix the cause and trigger a new embed. |

===FINDINGS_BEGIN===
[
  {
    "id": "F1",
    "severity": "P1",
    "file": ".claude/mcp.json",
    "line": "45",
    "title": "The MK hook-disable rename is not honored end-to-end",
    "why": "The four runtime configs are aligned on MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED, but the skill-advisor launcher/daemon paths still forward/read only SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED. Dropping the legacy config key makes a config-level disable toggle ineffective outside the OpenCode plugin path.",
    "evidence": ".claude/mcp.json:45, .mcp.json:45, opencode.json:56, and .codex/config.toml:109 set only MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED. .opencode/plugins/mk-skill-advisor.js:38 and :82-86 honor MK plus legacy names, but .opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs:85-129 allowlists SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED and not MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED; .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/handlers/advisor-recommend.ts:414 reads process.env.SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED only.",
    "recommendation": "Either keep SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED in the runtime configs until all launcher/daemon/hook consumers accept MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED, or add the MK alias to the launcher allowlist and all disable checks before removing the legacy key."
  }
]
===FINDINGS_END===
